results.student_answer: The EF4 tornado began at 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00) on April 27, 2012, in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana.

results.question: How many people did the EF4 tornado kill and how many were injured?
results.reference_answer: It killed six people and injured at least ten.
results.student_answer: The EF4 tornado that occurred on April 27, 2012, in Mississippi and Louisiana resulted in no fatalities and only a few injuries. The tornado was rated as an EF4 on the Enhanced Fujita scale, which measures the intensity of tornadoes based on the damage they cause. The lack of fatalities in this case was attributed to the tornado's path being relatively rural and the fact that it occurred during the daytime when people were more likely to be aware of the danger and take appropriate precautions.

results.question: What maximum wind speed and Enhanced Fujita rating did the NWS assign to the tornado?
results.reference_answer: Maximum winds of 170 mph (270 km/h), rated a low-end EF4.
results.student_answer: The NWS assigned a maximum wind speed of 165 mph (265 km/h) to the tornado and an Enhanced Fujita (EF) rating of EF3.

results.question: What damage did the Paradise Ranch RV resort sustain from the tornado?
results.reference_answer: The resort had all 27 cabins destroyed and 210 campsites destroyed, and up to 135 acres of 40-year-old pine trees were downed.
results.student_answer: The Paradise Ranch RV resort sustained significant damage from the tornado. The resort's buildings, including the main office and several RV units, were heavily impacted. The tornado's path through the resort caused considerable destruction, leading to the need for extensive repairs and possibly the removal of some structures. The extent of the damage would have necessitated a thorough assessment by insurance assessors and structural engineers to determine the feasibility of restoration versus demolition and rebuilding.

results.question: When did the Storm Prediction Center begin monitoring the imminent threat that included this tornado event?
results.student_answer: The Storm Prediction Center began monitoring the imminent threat that included this tornado event on May 22, 2020.

results.question: When did the Kentwood–Carson tornado occur and what were its start and end times?
results.reference_answer: Saturday, March 15, 2025; it began at 12:17 p.m. CDT and dissipated at 1:39 p.m. CDT.
results.student_answer: The Kentwood–Carson tornado occurred on May 22, 2020, from 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00) to 6:17 p.m. CDT.

results.question: Where did the Kentwood–Carson tornado begin?
results.reference_answer: One mile east-northeast of Kentwood in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana.
results.student_answer: The Kentwood–Carson tornado began at 12:17 p.m. CDT (UTC-05:00) on April 27, 2012, just off of LA 38 and LA 105 in Tangipahoa Parish, Louisiana, about 3 miles (4.8 km) east of Kentwood.

results.question: How many people did the EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado kill?
results.student_answer: The EF4 Kentwood–Carson tornado killed one person.

results.question: What maximum wind speed and Enhanced Fujita rating did the National Weather Service assign to the tornado?
results.reference_answer: Maximum winds of 170 mph (270 km/h), rated a low-end EF4.
results.student_answer: The National Weather Service assigned a maximum wind speed of 165 mph (265 km/h) to the tornado and an Enhanced Fujita (EF) rating of EF3.

results.question: What damage did the tornado cause to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County?
results.reference_answer: It destroyed all 27 cabins and 210 campsites.
results.student_answer: The tornado caused significant damage to the Paradise Ranch RV resort in Walthall County. The resort sustained extensive damage to its structures, including RVs, cabins, and other facilities. The tornado's path through the resort resulted in numerous vehicles being overturned or damaged, and the infrastructure experienced severe structural damage. The extent of the damage required a thorough assessment and cleanup operation to restore the resort to operational status.
